disrupt.en: interrupt the normal progress of something

ex.en: The strike disrupted train services across the country.
ex.ko: 파업으로 전국 기차 운행에 차질이 빚어졌다.

dissatisfaction.en: a feeling of not being satisfied or pleased

ex.en: There is growing dissatisfaction with the new policy.
ex.ko: 새 정책에 대한 불만이 커지고 있다.

dissatisfied.en: not pleased or happy with something

ex.en: Many customers were dissatisfied with the service.
ex.ko: 많은 고객들이 그 서비스에 불만족스러워했다.

dissident.en: a person who publicly opposes an official policy or system, especially in a country with strict controls

ex.en: The dissident was arrested for criticizing the government.
ex.ko: 그 반체제 인사는 정부를 비판했다는 이유로 체포되었다.

dissident.en: opposing official policy or an established system

ex.en: Dissident voices within the party were silenced.
ex.ko: 당 내 반체제 목소리는 잠잠해졌다.

ex.en: She is a distinguished professor of physics.
ex.ko: 그녀는 저명한 물리학 교수이다.

distinguished.en: having an appearance or manner that shows dignity and elegance

ex.en: He had a distinguished, gray-haired look.
ex.ko: 그는 백발의 기품 있는 모습이었다.

distortion.en: a change that makes something no longer true or accurate

ex.en: The report was a distortion of the facts.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 사실을 왜곡한 것이었다.

distortion.en: a change in the shape or sound of something that makes it different from normal

ex.en: The old speaker produced a lot of sound distortion.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 스피커는 소리 왜곡이 심했다.

distract.ko: 주의를 산만하게 하다, 딴 데로 돌리다
distract.en: take someone's attention away from what they are doing

ex.en: Loud noises distracted her from her homework.
ex.ko: 시끄러운 소리가 그녀의 숙제 집중을 방해했다.

distraction.en: something that takes your attention away from what you should be doing

ex.en: Phones are a major distraction in class.
ex.ko: 휴대폰은 수업 중 주요 방해 요소이다.

distraction.en: an activity that helps you relax and forget your worries

ex.en: Watching movies is a good distraction from stress.
ex.ko: 영화 보기는 스트레스에서 벗어나는 좋은 기분 전환거리이다.

distressing.en: making you feel upset, worried, or unhappy

ex.en: It was distressing to see the animals in such poor condition.
ex.ko: 그런 열악한 상태의 동물들을 보는 것은 괴로웠다.

distributor.en: a company or person that supplies goods to shops or other businesses

ex.en: The company hired a new distributor for Europe.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 유럽 담당 신규 유통업체를 고용했다.

diversion.en: an activity that provides amusement or relaxation

ex.en: Reading is a pleasant diversion after a long day.
ex.ko: 독서는 힘든 하루를 보낸 후의 즐거운 기분 전환거리이다.

diversion.en: a change in the direction or route of something, especially traffic

ex.en: There's a diversion on the motorway due to roadworks.
ex.ko: 도로 공사로 고속도로에 우회로가 생겼다.

divert.en: make something change direction or route

ex.en: The flight was diverted to another airport due to bad weather.
ex.ko: 그 항공편은 악천후로 다른 공항으로 우회했다.

divert.en: secretly move money to a different, often dishonest, use

ex.en: He was accused of diverting company funds into his own account.
ex.ko: 그는 회사 자금을 자신의 계좌로 빼돌린 혐의를 받았다.

divided.en: split into separate parts, or having very different opinions

ex.en: The committee was divided over the new proposal.
ex.ko: 위원회는 새 제안을 두고 의견이 갈렸다.

division.en: a large part of an organization, business, or government

ex.en: She works in the marketing division of the company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 회사의 마케팅 부서에서 일한다.

division.en: the act of separating something into parts, or a serious disagreement between people

ex.en: The war caused deep division within the country.
ex.ko: 그 전쟁은 나라 안에 깊은 분열을 일으켰다.

division.en: the process of dividing one number by another

ex.en: Children learn division after multiplication.
ex.ko: 아이들은 곱셈 다음에 나눗셈을 배운다.

divorce.en: the legal ending of a marriage

ex.en: Their divorce was finalized last month.
ex.ko: 그들의 이혼은 지난달에 마무리되었다.

ex.en: They decided to divorce after ten years of marriage.
ex.ko: 그들은 결혼 10년 만에 이혼하기로 결정했다.

dna.en: the substance in the cells of living things that carries genetic information

ex.en: Scientists analyzed the DNA sample from the crime scene.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 범죄 현장에서 나온 DNA 샘플을 분석했다.

dock.en: a place where ships load and unload goods or passengers

ex.en: The ship arrived at the dock early in the morning.
ex.ko: 그 배는 이른 아침 부두에 도착했다.

dock.en: (of a ship) come into a harbor and stop at a dock

ex.en: The ferry docks here twice a day.
ex.ko: 그 여객선은 하루에 두 번 이곳에 정박한다.

dock.en: take away part of someone's money or benefits as a punishment

ex.en: His pay was docked for being late.
ex.ko: 그는 지각 때문에 임금이 깎였다.

domestic.en: relating to a particular country's internal affairs, rather than other countries

ex.en: The president focused on domestic policy this year.
ex.ko: 대통령은 올해 국내 정책에 집중했다.

domestic.en: relating to the home or family life

ex.en: She was overwhelmed by domestic chores.
ex.ko: 그녀는 집안일에 지쳐 있었다.

dominate.en: have control over or be the most powerful or important part of something

ex.en: The company dominates the smartphone market.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 스마트폰 시장을 지배하고 있다.

donate.en: give money or goods to help a person or cause

ex.en: She donated her old clothes to charity.
ex.ko: 그녀는 헌 옷을 자선단체에 기부했다.

donation.en: money or goods given to help a person or cause

ex.en: The charity relies on public donations.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 대중의 기부에 의존한다.

donkey.en: an animal like a small horse with long ears, used to carry loads

ex.en: The farmer used a donkey to carry the goods.
ex.ko: 농부는 당나귀를 이용해 짐을 날랐다.

doom.en: a very bad fate, especially death or destruction

ex.en: The village faced doom as the flood approached.
ex.ko: 홍수가 다가오자 마을은 파멸의 위기에 처했다.

doom.cue: 안 좋은 결말을 피할 수 없게
doom.en: make something certain to fail or end badly

ex.en: The plan was doomed to failure from the start.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 처음부터 실패할 운명이었다.

doorkeeper.en: a person whose job is to guard or watch an entrance

ex.en: The doorkeeper checked everyone's tickets before letting them in.
ex.ko: 문지기는 입장시키기 전에 모두의 표를 확인했다.

doorway.en: the space in a wall where a door is, through which you enter a room or building

ex.en: She stood in the doorway, waiting for a reply.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대답을 기다리며 출입구에 서 있었다.

dorm.en: a building where students live at a college or university (informal)

ex.en: I lived in a dorm during my freshman year.
ex.ko: 나는 대학 1학년 때 기숙사에서 살았다.

dormitory.en: a building at a college or university where students live

ex.en: She stayed in the dormitory during the semester.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학기 동안 기숙사에서 지냈다.

dormitory.cue: 침대 여러 개가 있는 공동 침실
dormitory.en: a large room with several beds, especially in a school or hostel

ex.en: The hostel had a dormitory with twelve beds.
ex.ko: 그 호스텔에는 침대 12개가 있는 공동 침실이 있었다.

dose.en: an amount of medicine taken at one time

ex.en: Take one dose of the medicine every morning.
ex.ko: 매일 아침 이 약을 한 번씩 복용하세요.

dose.en: give someone a measured amount of medicine

ex.en: The vet dosed the dog with antibiotics.
ex.ko: 수의사는 개에게 항생제를 투여했다.

doubly.en: to an even greater degree; twice as much

ex.en: Her achievement is doubly impressive given her age.
ex.ko: 그녀의 나이를 고려하면 그 성취는 더욱더 인상적이다.

doubtful.en: not likely to be true, or not certain to happen

ex.en: It's doubtful that they will arrive on time.
ex.ko: 그들이 제시간에 도착할지 의심스럽다.

ex.en: She looked doubtful about the plan.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 계획에 대해 확신하지 못하는 표정이었다.

dove.en: a white or gray bird similar to a pigeon, often a symbol of peace

ex.en: They released doves at the peace ceremony.
ex.ko: 그들은 평화 기념식에서 비둘기를 날려 보냈다.

dove.en: a person who prefers peaceful negotiation over military action in politics

ex.en: The doves in the cabinet argued against military action.
ex.ko: 내각의 비둘기파는 군사 행동에 반대했다.

downfall.en: a sudden loss of power, status, or success

ex.en: Greed was the downfall of the company.
ex.ko: 탐욕이 그 회사의 몰락을 불러왔다.

downsize.en: (of a company) reduce the number of employees or the size of operations

ex.en: The firm downsized its workforce by 20 percent.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 인력을 20퍼센트 감원했다.

dowry.cue: 결혼할 때 신부 측이 주는 재산
dowry.en: money or property that a woman's family gives to her husband when they marry

ex.en: In the past, a bride's family often paid a dowry.
ex.ko: 과거에는 신부 측 가족이 지참금을 주는 경우가 많았다.

draft.en: an early version of a piece of writing that is not yet finished

ex.en: I wrote a first draft of my essay last night.
ex.ko: 나는 어젯밤 에세이 초안을 썼다.

draft.en: prepare an early written version of a plan or document

ex.en: The lawyer drafted a new contract.
ex.ko: 변호사는 새 계약서 초안을 작성했다.

draft.en: a system requiring people to join the military

ex.en: He was called up in the draft during the war.
ex.ko: 그는 전쟁 중 징병으로 소집되었다.

draft.en: a current of cold air blowing into a room

ex.en: There's a draft coming from under the door.
ex.ko: 문 밑에서 외풍이 들어온다.

drain.en: (of liquid) flow away, or make liquid flow away from something

ex.en: Drain the pasta before adding the sauce.
ex.ko: 소스를 넣기 전에 파스타의 물을 빼세요.

drain.en: gradually use up someone's money, energy, or resources

ex.en: The long project drained all our energy.
ex.ko: 그 긴 프로젝트는 우리의 기력을 모두 소모시켰다.

drain.en: a pipe or channel that carries away waste water

ex.en: The drain in the bathroom is blocked.
ex.ko: 욕실 배수구가 막혔다.

dramatically.en: suddenly and to a great extent

ex.en: Sales increased dramatically after the ad campaign.
ex.ko: 광고 캠페인 이후 매출이 급격히 늘었다.

dramatically.en: in a way that is exaggerated or theatrical

ex.en: He gasped dramatically and covered his face.
ex.ko: 그는 과장되게 숨을 헐떡이며 얼굴을 가렸다.

drapery.en: heavy cloth hung as a curtain or decoration

ex.en: Thick drapery covered the tall windows.
ex.ko: 두꺼운 커튼이 높은 창문을 가리고 있었다.

dread.en: feel great fear or worry about something that is going to happen

ex.en: I dread going to the dentist.
ex.ko: 나는 치과에 가는 게 몹시 두렵다.

dread.en: a feeling of great fear about something that may happen

ex.en: She felt a sense of dread before the exam results.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시험 결과를 앞두고 두려움을 느꼈다.

ex.en: The weather was dreadful all week.
ex.ko: 그 주 내내 날씨가 지독했다.

dreamy.en: seeming to be thinking about something else, not paying attention to what is real

ex.en: She had a dreamy expression on her face.
ex.ko: 그녀의 얼굴에는 몽환적인 표정이 떠올라 있었다.

ex.en: Is the tap water here drinkable?
ex.ko: 여기 수돗물은 마실 수 있나요?

droop.en: bend or hang down, especially from weakness or tiredness

ex.en: Her eyelids drooped as she grew sleepy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 졸려서 눈꺼풀이 축 늘어졌다.

dropout.cue: 학교를 끝까지 다니지 않고 그만둔 사람
dropout.en: someone who leaves school before finishing their studies

ex.en: He became a successful entrepreneur despite being a college dropout.
ex.ko: 그는 대학 중퇴자였지만 성공한 사업가가 되었다.

drought.en: a long period of little or no rain

ex.en: The region suffered a severe drought last summer.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 지난여름 심한 가뭄을 겪었다.

duel.cue: 명예를 걸고 벌이는 대결(또는 치열한 경쟁)
duel.en: a formal fight between two people, or an intense contest between two rivals

ex.en: The two chess masters engaged in a mental duel.
ex.ko: 두 체스 마스터는 두뇌 대결을 벌였다.

ex.en: That was a dumb mistake to make.
ex.ko: 그건 정말 멍청한 실수였다.

dumb.en: temporarily unable to speak, especially from shock

ex.en: She was struck dumb with surprise.
ex.ko: 그녀는 놀라서 말문이 막혔다.

ex.en: Keep a duplicate of this key in a safe place.
ex.ko: 이 열쇠의 사본을 안전한 곳에 보관하세요.

duplicate.en: make an exact copy of something

ex.en: The results were duplicated in a second experiment.
ex.ko: 그 결과는 두 번째 실험에서도 똑같이 재현되었다.

duplication.en: the act of copying something, or of doing the same thing more than once unnecessarily

ex.en: There was a lot of duplication of effort between the two teams.
ex.ko: 두 팀 사이에 노력의 중복이 많았다.

dwarf.cue: 비교했을 때 아주 작아 보이게 하다
dwarf.en: make something seem small by comparison

ex.en: The skyscraper dwarfs all the other buildings nearby.
ex.ko: 그 마천루는 주변의 다른 건물들을 왜소해 보이게 한다.

dwarf.en: a legendary creature that looks like a small old man, or a very small person

ex.en: The story tells of seven dwarfs living in the forest.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 숲에 사는 일곱 난쟁이에 대한 것이다.

dye.en: a substance used to change the color of something, such as cloth or hair

ex.en: She used a natural dye to color the fabric.
ex.ko: 그녀는 천연 염료로 천을 물들였다.

dye.en: change the color of something using a dye

ex.en: He dyed his hair black.
ex.ko: 그는 머리를 검게 염색했다.

dynamic.en: full of energy and new ideas; constantly changing or developing

ex.en: She has a dynamic personality that inspires others.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다른 사람들에게 영감을 주는 역동적인 성격을 가지고 있다.

eagerly.en: in a way that shows strong interest or desire to do something

ex.en: The children waited eagerly for the show to start.
ex.ko: 아이들은 공연이 시작하기를 간절히 기다렸다.

eagle.en: a large bird of prey with excellent eyesight and strong wings

ex.en: An eagle soared high above the mountains.
ex.ko: 독수리 한 마리가 산 위로 높이 날아올랐다.

earnings.en: the money that a person earns from working

ex.en: His earnings increased after the promotion.
ex.ko: 승진 후 그의 수입이 늘었다.

earnings.en: the profit that a company makes

ex.en: The company reported record earnings this quarter.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이번 분기 사상 최대 수익을 발표했다.

ease.en: the state of being free from difficulty, effort, or worry

ex.en: She solved the puzzle with ease.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 퍼즐을 쉽게 풀었다.

ease.en: make pain, worry, or difficulty less severe

ex.en: The medicine eased her pain quickly.
ex.ko: 그 약은 그녀의 통증을 빠르게 완화해 주었다.

ex.en: The birds migrated eastward for the winter.
ex.ko: 그 새들은 겨울을 나기 위해 동쪽으로 이동했다.

ecologically.en: with respect to the relationship between living things and their environment

ex.en: The farm operates in an ecologically sustainable way.
ex.ko: 그 농장은 생태학적으로 지속 가능한 방식으로 운영된다.

ecologist.en: a scientist who studies the relationships between living things and their environment

ex.en: The ecologist studied how pollution affected the river.
ex.ko: 그 생태학자는 오염이 강에 미치는 영향을 연구했다.

economical.en: giving good value or not wasting money, time, or resources

ex.en: This car is economical to run.
ex.ko: 이 차는 유지비가 경제적이다.

economist.en: an expert in the study of economics

ex.en: The economist predicted a slow recovery.
ex.ko: 그 경제학자는 더딘 회복을 예측했다.

ecstatic.en: feeling or expressing extreme happiness or excitement

ex.en: She was ecstatic when she got the acceptance letter.
ex.ko: 그녀는 합격 통지서를 받고 너무 기뻐 어쩔 줄 몰라했다.

edit.en: prepare written or recorded material by correcting, changing, or removing parts

ex.en: She edited the article before it was published.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기사가 게재되기 전에 편집했다.

edit.en: a change made to a piece of writing or recording

ex.en: He made a few small edits to the document.
ex.ko: 그는 문서에 몇 가지 사소한 수정을 했다.

editorial.en: a newspaper or magazine article that gives the writer's or publisher's opinion

ex.en: The newspaper published an editorial on climate change.
ex.ko: 그 신문은 기후 변화에 대한 사설을 실었다.

editorial.en: relating to the process of editing or to editors

ex.en: She has final editorial control over the magazine.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 잡지에 대한 최종 편집권을 가지고 있다.

educated.en: having had a lot of schooling or knowledge

ex.en: She comes from a highly educated family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학식이 높은 집안 출신이다.

educated.en: based on knowledge or reasonable information, not just a guess

ex.en: It was an educated guess based on past data.
ex.ko: 그것은 과거 데이터에 근거한 추측이었다.

educator.en: a person who teaches or works in education

ex.en: She has been an educator for over twenty years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 20년 넘게 교육자로 일해 왔다.

effectively.en: in a way that produces the result you want

ex.en: She communicated her ideas effectively.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 생각을 효과적으로 전달했다.

effectively.en: in reality, even if not officially or exactly

ex.en: The company was effectively bankrupt.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 사실상 파산 상태였다.

effectiveness.en: the degree to which something produces the intended result

ex.en: They questioned the effectiveness of the new treatment.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 새 치료법의 효과성에 의문을 제기했다.

elect.en: choose someone for a position by voting

ex.en: They elected a new president last year.
ex.ko: 그들은 작년에 새 대통령을 선출했다.

elect.en: choose to do something, especially after careful thought (formal)

ex.en: She elected to stay home rather than travel.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행 대신 집에 있기로 선택했다.

elective.en: a course that students can choose to take, rather than one that is required

ex.en: I took photography as an elective this semester.
ex.ko: 나는 이번 학기에 사진을 선택 과목으로 들었다.

elective.en: not compulsory; able to be chosen

ex.en: Attendance at the seminar is elective, not mandatory.
ex.ko: 그 세미나 참석은 필수가 아니라 선택 사항이다.

electrician.en: a person whose job is to install and repair electrical systems

ex.en: We called an electrician to fix the wiring.
ex.ko: 우리는 배선을 고치려고 전기 기술자를 불렀다.

electronically.en: by using electronic equipment or systems

ex.en: The documents were sent electronically.
ex.ko: 그 서류들은 전자적으로 전송되었다.

electronics.en: the branch of science and technology dealing with electronic devices and circuits

ex.en: He studied electronics at university.
ex.ko: 그는 대학에서 전자공학을 공부했다.

electronics.en: electronic devices such as computers, phones, and televisions

ex.en: The store sells a wide range of electronics.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 다양한 전자제품을 판매한다.

elegance.en: the quality of being graceful and stylish in appearance or manner

ex.en: The dress was admired for its simple elegance.
ex.ko: 그 드레스는 소박한 우아함으로 찬사를 받았다.

elegant.en: graceful and stylish in appearance, manner, or design

ex.en: She wore an elegant black dress to the gala.
ex.ko: 그녀는 갈라 행사에 우아한 검은 드레스를 입고 갔다.

elegant.en: (of a solution or idea) simple, clever, and effective

ex.en: The mathematician found an elegant proof for the theorem.
ex.ko: 그 수학자는 그 정리에 대한 간결하고 뛰어난 증명을 찾아냈다.

elegantly.en: in a graceful and stylish way

ex.en: She danced elegantly across the stage.
ex.ko: 그녀는 무대 위에서 우아하게 춤을 췄다.

elemental.en: forming an essential or basic part of something

ex.en: Trust is an elemental part of any relationship.
ex.ko: 신뢰는 모든 관계의 근본적인 부분이다.

eligible.en: having the necessary qualifications to do or receive something

ex.en: You must be over 18 to be eligible to vote.
ex.ko: 투표할 자격이 있으려면 18세가 넘어야 한다.

elite.en: a small group of people who have the most power, wealth, or talent in a society

ex.en: Only the elite could afford such luxury.
ex.ko: 오직 상류층만이 그런 사치를 누릴 수 있었다.

elite.en: belonging to the group considered the best or most powerful

ex.en: She trains with an elite team of athletes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 엘리트 운동선수 팀과 함께 훈련한다.

eloquence.en: the ability to speak or write in a powerful and persuasive way

ex.en: His eloquence moved the entire audience.
ex.ko: 그의 웅변은 청중 전체를 감동시켰다.

eloquent.en: able to express ideas clearly and persuasively

ex.en: She gave an eloquent speech at the ceremony.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 기념식에서 유창한 연설을 했다.

embassy.en: the official residence or offices of an ambassador in a foreign country

ex.en: She works at the embassy in Seoul.
ex.ko: 그녀는 서울에 있는 대사관에서 근무한다.

embrace.en: the act of putting your arms around someone to show love or affection

ex.en: They shared a warm embrace at the airport.
ex.ko: 그들은 공항에서 따뜻한 포옹을 나눴다.

embrace.en: put your arms around someone to show love or affection

ex.en: She embraced her son tightly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아들을 꼭 껴안았다.

embrace.en: accept a new idea, belief, or way of doing something enthusiastically

ex.en: The company embraced new technology quickly.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새로운 기술을 빠르게 받아들였다.

emergence.en: the process of coming into existence or becoming known

ex.en: The emergence of social media changed communication.
ex.ko: 소셜미디어의 등장은 소통 방식을 바꾸었다.

emphatically.en: in a forceful and definite way, leaving no doubt

ex.en: She emphatically denied the accusation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 혐의를 단호하게 부인했다.

employ.en: pay someone to work for you

ex.en: The factory employs over 200 workers.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 200명이 넘는 근로자를 고용하고 있다.

employ.en: use a particular method, skill, or object for a purpose

ex.en: The artist employed bold colors in her work.
ex.ko: 그 화가는 자신의 작품에 대담한 색을 활용했다.

employee.en: a person who is paid to work for someone

ex.en: The company has over 500 employees.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 500명이 넘는 직원을 두고 있다.

employer.en: a person or company that employs workers

ex.en: Her employer offered her a raise.
ex.ko: 그녀의 고용주는 그녀에게 임금 인상을 제안했다.

endeavor.en: a serious attempt to do or achieve something

ex.en: Their scientific endeavor led to a major discovery.
ex.ko: 그들의 과학적 노력은 큰 발견으로 이어졌다.

endeavor.en: try hard to do or achieve something

ex.en: We will endeavor to finish the project on time.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내려고 노력할 것이다.

endlessly.en: in a way that seems to have no end; continuously

ex.en: He talked endlessly about his trip.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 여행에 대해 끝없이 이야기했다.

endorse.en: publicly say that you support a person, plan, or statement

ex.en: The mayor endorsed the new education plan.
ex.ko: 시장은 새 교육 계획을 지지했다.

endorse.en: (of a famous person) say publicly that you like and use a product, to encourage others to buy it

ex.en: The athlete endorses a popular sports brand.
ex.ko: 그 운동선수는 유명 스포츠 브랜드를 홍보한다.

endurance.en: the ability to keep doing something difficult or painful for a long time

ex.en: Marathon running requires great endurance.
ex.ko: 마라톤은 대단한 지구력을 필요로 한다.

enduring.en: continuing to exist for a long time

ex.en: They have an enduring friendship of thirty years.
ex.ko: 그들은 30년간 이어진 지속적인 우정을 갖고 있다.

energy.en: power obtained from sources like electricity, fuel, or food, used to do work

ex.en: Solar panels convert sunlight into energy.
ex.ko: 태양광 패널은 햇빛을 에너지로 바꾼다.

energy.cue: 사람이 활동할 수 있는 힘, 기운
energy.en: the physical strength and enthusiasm needed for activity

ex.en: She has so much energy in the mornings.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아침마다 활력이 넘친다.

enforce.en: make sure that a law or rule is obeyed

ex.en: The police enforce traffic laws strictly.
ex.ko: 경찰은 교통법을 엄격히 집행한다.

ex.en: They announced their engagement last week.
ex.ko: 그들은 지난주에 약혼을 발표했다.

engagement.en: an arrangement to meet someone or do something at a particular time

ex.en: She has a prior engagement this evening.
ex.ko: 그녀는 오늘 저녁 선약이 있다.

engagement.en: active involvement or participation in an activity

ex.en: The teacher encouraged student engagement in class discussions.
ex.ko: 교사는 학생들이 수업 토론에 적극 참여하도록 독려했다.

engulf.en: cover or surround something completely, especially suddenly

ex.en: Flames quickly engulfed the building.
ex.ko: 불길이 순식간에 건물을 휩쌌다.

enhance.en: improve the quality, value, or attractiveness of something

ex.en: The new software enhances the app's performance.
ex.ko: 그 새 소프트웨어는 앱의 성능을 향상시킨다.

enroll.en: officially register as a member of a course, school, or group

ex.en: She enrolled in a Spanish class this year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 올해 스페인어 수업에 등록했다.

enterprise.en: a company or business, especially a new one

ex.en: She runs a small enterprise selling handmade jewelry.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수제 장신구를 파는 작은 사업체를 운영한다.

enterprise.en: the ability and willingness to start new projects or businesses, especially involving risk

ex.en: His enterprise and hard work led to great success.
ex.ko: 그의 진취성과 노력이 큰 성공으로 이어졌다.

entertaining.en: enjoyable and amusing to watch, hear, or read

ex.en: The movie was surprisingly entertaining.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 의외로 재미있었다.

ex.en: The crowd cheered enthusiastically for the team.
ex.ko: 관중들은 그 팀을 열정적으로 응원했다.

entitle.en: give someone the right to have or do something

ex.en: This ticket entitles you to a free drink.
ex.ko: 이 티켓은 무료 음료 한 잔을 받을 자격을 준다.

entitle.en: give a title to a book, film, or other work

ex.en: He wrote an essay entitled 'The Future of Work.'
ex.ko: 그는 '일의 미래'라는 제목의 에세이를 썼다.

entrepreneur.en: a person who starts and runs a business, taking on financial risk

ex.en: She became a successful entrepreneur at a young age.
ex.ko: 그녀는 젊은 나이에 성공한 기업가가 되었다.

environment.en: the natural world or the conditions and surroundings in which someone lives or works

ex.en: We need to protect the environment.
ex.ko: 우리는 환경을 보호해야 한다.

environmentally.en: in a way that relates to or affects the environment

ex.en: The product is environmentally responsible.
ex.ko: 그 제품은 환경적으로 책임감 있게 만들어졌다.

ex.en: They switched to environmentally friendly packaging.
ex.ko: 그들은 친환경 포장재로 바꿨다.

equation.en: a mathematical statement showing that two expressions are equal

ex.en: She solved the equation quickly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 방정식을 빠르게 풀었다.

equation.en: the idea that two things are equal or closely linked

ex.en: There's a simple equation between hard work and success.
ex.ko: 노력과 성공 사이에는 단순한 등식이 있다.

equip.en: provide someone or something with the equipment needed for a particular purpose

ex.en: The lab is equipped with modern instruments.
ex.ko: 그 실험실은 최신 장비를 갖추고 있다.

equip.en: give someone the knowledge or skills needed for a task

ex.en: The course equips students with practical skills.
ex.ko: 그 과정은 학생들에게 실용적 기술을 갖추게 한다.

equity.en: the value of shares owned by shareholders in a company

ex.en: Investors hold equity in the startup.
ex.ko: 투자자들은 그 스타트업의 지분을 보유하고 있다.

equity.en: the quality of being fair and treating everyone equally

ex.en: The policy aims to promote equity in education.
ex.ko: 그 정책은 교육의 형평성을 증진하는 것을 목표로 한다.

ex.en: He stood erect with his shoulders back.
ex.ko: 그는 어깨를 펴고 똑바로 섰다.

erect.en: build or put up a structure

ex.en: Workers erected a new bridge over the river.
ex.ko: 인부들은 강 위에 새 다리를 세웠다.

erupt.en: (of a volcano) suddenly throw out lava, ash, and gas

ex.en: The volcano erupted after years of silence.
ex.ko: 그 화산은 수년간의 침묵 끝에 분출했다.

erupt.en: suddenly begin or become much more intense, especially violence or emotion

ex.en: Violence erupted after the match.
ex.ko: 경기 후 폭력 사태가 터졌다.

eruption.en: a sudden violent explosion of lava, ash, and gas from a volcano

ex.en: The eruption forced thousands to evacuate.
ex.ko: 그 화산 분출로 수천 명이 대피해야 했다.

eruption.en: a sudden and intense outburst of something, such as violence or emotion

ex.en: There was an eruption of anger among the fans.
ex.ko: 팬들 사이에서 분노가 폭발적으로 터져나왔다.

ex.en: He is a well-known essayist and critic.
ex.ko: 그는 유명한 수필가이자 평론가이다.

essentially.en: in the most basic or important respect

ex.en: The two plans are essentially the same.
ex.ko: 그 두 계획은 본질적으로 같다.

estate.en: all the money and property that someone owns, especially after they die

ex.en: He left his entire estate to his children.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 전 재산을 자녀들에게 남겼다.

estate.en: a large area of land in the countryside, often with a large house

ex.en: The family owns a large country estate.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 넓은 시골 사유지를 소유하고 있다.

ex.en: The estimated cost of the project is one million dollars.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트의 추정 비용은 100만 달러이다.

ex.en: She will be eternally grateful for their help.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그들의 도움에 영원히 감사할 것이다.

ethic.en: a set of moral principles that guide a person's or group's behavior

ex.en: He has a strong work ethic.
ex.ko: 그는 강한 직업윤리를 가지고 있다.

ethnic.en: relating to a group of people who share the same culture, language, or nationality

ex.en: The festival celebrates ethnic diversity.
ex.ko: 그 축제는 민족 다양성을 기념한다.

evacuate.cue: 위험한 곳에서 안전한 곳으로 떠나(게 하)다
evacuate.en: move people away from a dangerous place to somewhere safer

ex.en: Residents were evacuated before the storm hit.
ex.ko: 주민들은 폭풍이 닥치기 전에 대피했다.

evacuation.en: the act of moving people away from a dangerous place

ex.en: The evacuation of the town went smoothly.
ex.ko: 그 마을의 대피는 순조롭게 진행되었다.

evaluate.en: judge or calculate the quality, importance, or value of something

ex.en: Teachers evaluate students' progress every semester.
ex.ko: 교사들은 매 학기 학생들의 발전 정도를 평가한다.

evaluation.en: the process of judging or calculating the value or quality of something

ex.en: The performance evaluation is due next week.
ex.ko: 그 성과 평가는 다음 주에 마감이다.

everlasting.en: lasting forever or for a very long time

ex.en: They promised each other everlasting love.
ex.ko: 그들은 서로에게 영원한 사랑을 약속했다.

evidently.en: clearly or obviously, based on what can be seen

ex.en: Evidently, she had not read the instructions.
ex.ko: 보아하니 그녀는 설명서를 읽지 않은 게 분명했다.

evil.en: morally wrong and harmful; deeply immoral

ex.en: The story is about the battle between good and evil forces.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 선한 세력과 사악한 세력 간의 싸움에 관한 것이다.

evil.en: morally wrong behavior, or something that causes great harm

ex.en: Poverty is often described as a social evil.
ex.ko: 빈곤은 종종 사회적 악으로 묘사된다.

evolution.en: the gradual process by which living things change and develop over generations

ex.en: Darwin's theory of evolution changed science forever.
ex.ko: 다윈의 진화론은 과학을 영원히 바꿔놓았다.

evolution.en: the gradual development of something over time

ex.en: The evolution of technology has been rapid in recent decades.
ex.ko: 최근 수십 년간 기술의 발전은 빨랐다.

evolve.en: develop gradually over time, especially from a simpler to a more complex form

ex.en: The company has evolved into a global brand.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 글로벌 브랜드로 발전했다.

exactness.en: the quality of being exact and precise

ex.en: The scientist worked with great exactness.
ex.ko: 그 과학자는 대단한 정확성을 가지고 작업했다.

exaggerate.en: describe something as bigger, better, or worse than it really is

ex.en: He tends to exaggerate his achievements.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 업적을 과장하는 경향이 있다.

exaggeration.en: a statement that makes something seem bigger, better, or worse than it really is

ex.en: Saying it was the best day ever is not an exaggeration.
ex.ko: 그날이 최고의 날이었다고 말하는 건 과장이 아니다.

exalt.en: praise or honor someone or something highly

ex.en: The poem exalts the beauty of nature.
ex.ko: 그 시는 자연의 아름다움을 찬양한다.

exalt.en: raise someone to a higher rank or position

ex.en: He was exalted to the rank of general.
ex.ko: 그는 장군의 지위로 승격되었다.

examinee.en: a person who takes an examination or test

ex.en: All examinees must arrive thirty minutes early.
ex.ko: 모든 응시자는 30분 일찍 도착해야 한다.

exceed.en: be greater than a particular number or amount, or go beyond what is allowed

ex.en: The speed exceeded the legal limit.
ex.ko: 그 속도는 법정 제한을 초과했다.

excel.en: be exceptionally good at doing something

ex.en: She excels in mathematics and science.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수학과 과학에서 뛰어나다.

exception.en: a person or thing that is not included in a general rule or pattern

ex.en: Most rules have an exception.
ex.ko: 대부분의 규칙에는 예외가 있다.

ex.en: She showed exceptional talent in music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 음악에 있어 탁월한 재능을 보여줬다.

ex.en: Except in exceptional circumstances, the rule applies to everyone.
ex.ko: 예외적인 상황이 아니면 그 규칙은 모두에게 적용된다.

ex.en: It was an exceptionally cold winter.
ex.ko: 유난히 추운 겨울이었다.

excessive.en: more than what is necessary, normal, or acceptable

ex.en: He was fined for excessive speeding.
ex.ko: 그는 과속으로 벌금을 물었다.

exclaim.en: say something suddenly and loudly, especially from surprise or strong emotion

ex.en: "That's amazing!" she exclaimed.
ex.ko: "정말 놀랍다!" 그녀가 외쳤다.

exclamation mark.en: the punctuation mark (!) used after a sentence to show strong feeling

ex.en: She ended the sentence with an exclamation mark.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 문장을 느낌표로 끝냈다.

exclude.en: deliberately not include or allow something or someone

ex.en: Children under 12 are excluded from this event.
ex.ko: 12세 미만 어린이는 이 행사에서 제외된다.

exclusion.en: the act of not including or allowing someone or something

ex.en: The exclusion of certain groups caused controversy.
ex.ko: 특정 집단의 배제는 논란을 일으켰다.

exclusively.en: only, and not involving anyone or anything else

ex.en: This offer is exclusively for members.
ex.ko: 이 혜택은 오로지 회원만을 위한 것이다.

excursion.en: a short trip taken for pleasure or a specific purpose

ex.en: We went on a day excursion to the coast.
ex.ko: 우리는 해안으로 당일 소풍을 다녀왔다.

execute.en: kill someone as a legal punishment

ex.en: The prisoner was executed at dawn.
ex.ko: 그 죄수는 새벽에 처형되었다.

execute.en: carry out or perform a plan, action, or task

ex.en: The team executed the plan perfectly.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 계획을 완벽하게 실행했다.

execution.en: the act of killing someone as a legal punishment

ex.en: The execution was carried out at dawn.
ex.ko: 처형은 새벽에 집행되었다.

execution.en: the act of carrying out or performing a plan or task

ex.en: The success of the project depends on careful execution.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트의 성공은 신중한 실행에 달려 있다.

executive.en: a person who has a senior managerial role in a business

ex.en: She is a senior executive at a tech company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한 기술 회사의 고위 임원이다.

executive.en: relating to managing a business or carrying out plans and decisions

ex.en: He was promoted to an executive position.
ex.ko: 그는 경영직으로 승진했다.

executive.en: the branch of government responsible for putting laws into effect

ex.en: The executive has the power to veto laws.
ex.ko: 행정부는 법률에 대한 거부권을 가진다.

ex.en: The long hike exhausted all of us.
ex.ko: 긴 하이킹은 우리 모두를 지치게 했다.

exhaust.en: use up all of a supply of something

ex.en: They exhausted their savings within a year.
ex.ko: 그들은 1년 만에 저축을 다 써버렸다.

exhaust.en: the gases released from a vehicle's engine

ex.en: The exhaust from old cars pollutes the air.
ex.ko: 낡은 차에서 나오는 배기가스는 공기를 오염시킨다.
